prerequisites: Lowercase powershellParser struct with pwshBin string and timeout time.Duration fields, plus NewPowerShellParser() constructor.
ParseResult{Classes []ClassDecl, Methods []MethodDecl, Imports []Import} with MethodDecl{QualifiedName, EnclosingClass, ReceiverCalls} and Import{Module, LangMeta map[string]any}.
Sentinel ErrParserUnavailable plus UnavailableError{Reason string} for errors.As-based reason extraction.
Helper isRelativeImport(string) bool visible in package ast.

phases.title: PowerShell fixture test
phases.summary: Stage 6.3 acceptance suite for the powershellParser subprocess parser. Untagged test file, parser-level assertions, pwsh-gated fixture cases skip on PowerShell-less CI.

stages.title: Scaffold and sentinel coverage (no pwsh required)
stages.summary: Create the untagged test file with file-local helpers and the only required test that runs on every CI host regardless of pwsh availability.

steps.title: Scaffold untagged test file and add TestPowerShellParser_NoPwsh_ReturnsSentinel
steps.summary: Create services/agent-memory/internal/repoindexer/ast/parser_powershell_test.go with no build tags and the file-local helpers classNames, methodNames, containsString, langMetaString, importModules. Add TestPowerShellParser_NoPwsh_ReturnsSentinel that constructs &powershellParser{pwshBin:""} directly, asserts errors.Is(err, ErrParserUnavailable) is true, and uses errors.As to read UnavailableError.Reason == "pwsh_not_available" (structural; do NOT substring-match err.Error()).

stages.title: Fixture-driven acceptance (pwsh-gated)
stages.summary: Tests that need pwsh on PATH to execute the embedded fixtures end-to-end. Each test opens with exec.LookPath("pwsh") + t.Skip so PowerShell-less CI stays green.

steps.title: Add TestPowerShellFixture_EmitsExpectedNodeAndEdgeSet
steps.summary: Embed the brief's Greeter fixture verbatim (class Greeter { [string] $Prefix; [string] Format([string]$name) { return "$($this.Prefix) $name" } [string] Greet([string]$name) { return $this.Format($name) } } function Format-Hello { param([string]$Name) return "hi $Name" } Import-Module Foo). Assert: 1 class node (Greeter); 3 method nodes (Greeter.Format, Greeter.Greet, Format-Hello) with EnclosingClass driving file-vs-class contains edges; 1 imports edge to Foo with LangMeta[module_kind]==Import-Module; Greeter.Greet.ReceiverCalls contains "Format" (drives Pass 2b static_calls Greeter.Greet -> Greeter.Format via the $this.X(...) extractor, tech-spec Section 5.6) AND does NOT contain "Format-Hello" (negative-resolution guard). [Greeter]::Format(...) static-class invocations are explicitly out of v1 scope per the brief; the fixture uses an instance receiver call.

steps.title: Add TestPowerShellFixture_DotSourceDropped
steps.summary: Per implementation-plan.md Stage 6.3 third bullet. Embed a fixture with `. ./helpers.ps1` dot-source. Assert the parser surfaces it in Imports with Module=="./helpers.ps1", LangMeta[module_kind]=="dot_source", and isRelativeImport(dot.Module)==true so the dispatcher suppresses the import edge (architecture Section 6.1 dot-source row).

outOfScope: Refactor of parser_powershell.go, dispatcher.go, or any production source under services/agent-memory/internal/repoindexer/ast/.
Dispatcher-vocabulary end-to-end assertions (Node/Edge capture via fakeNodeEdgeWriter) — lives in parser_powershell_dispatcher_test.go under //go:build canonical_dispatcher.
[Greeter]::Format(...) static-class invocation support — explicitly out of v1 per the brief.
Repo-wide baseline compile-error fixes (duplicate decls, missing lib/pq / pgx deps, parser_powershell.go full subprocess body) — tracked as separate workstreams.
